claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex. He's 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to ask:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(For children over 16, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if they are under 20 and staying in approved education or training.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and hasn't gone on to do anything else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. I have a few more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, how many hours per week does he work?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not working at all right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One more important question:

**Is Alex currently receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one final question to complete the assessment:

**Do you live in the UK and does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - we're in Manchester. And yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Eligibility Decision

I'm sorry, but **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: While you normally qualify for Child Benefit for children under 16, Alex is 18 years old. For children aged 16 or over, Child Benefit can continue if the child is under 20 **and** stays in approved education or training. Since Alex has finished school and is not currently in any education or training, he doesn't meet this requirement.

**However, there may be an option:** If Alex is 16 or 17 years old and has recently left education or training, you can apply for Child Benefit to continue for up to 20 weeks if he registers with either a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. Since Alex is 18, this temporary provision wouldn't apply either.

If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future while still under 20, you would be able to claim Child Benefit again at that point.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old, which is over the age of 16. According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. However, Alex is not currently in any approved education or training. He has finished school and is not doing anything else. While he is not working and not receiving benefits in his own right (which would have disqualified him), the key issue is that he does not meet the requirement of being in approved education or training as a 16-19 year old. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex in Manchester. While the basic residence requirements are met, Alex does not qualify because he is over 16 and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit for children over 16 requires them to be under 20 and in approved education or training, which Alex is not.

According to the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. However, Alex is not currently in any approved education or training. He has finished school and is not doing anything else. While he is not working and not receiving benefits in his own right (which would have disqualified him), the key issue is that he does not meet the requirement of being in approved education or training as a 16-19 year old. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with their 18-year-old son Alex in Manchester. While the basic residence requirements are met, Alex does not qualify because he is over 16 and not in approved education or training. Child Benefit for children over 16 requires them to be under 20 and in approved education or training, which Alex is not.
